location.office_time_required: up to one day per week from home (primarily in-office)

level_signals: Associate Analyst program is a three-to-five-year structured program
Designed to develop future fundamental investment analysts
Comp range $110K-$160K base, consistent with junior/associate level at asset manager

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's Degree with a strong academic record required
Strong Excel and financial modelling skills including previous experience building three-statement models

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strong academic background and intellectual curiosity
Highly motivated, confident self-starter, with ability to work independently and collaboratively in a performance-driven environment
A demonstrated interest and passion for equity investing and financial markets
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Demonstrated ability to derive meaningful insights from in-depth research and analysis
Preference for candidates who have made progress toward the CFA designation
Familiarity with LLMs, Claude Code, and/or Python is preferred

explicitly_excluded: FINRA licenses are not required and will not be supported for this role.

scope.surface: Large-Cap Growth Strategy — fundamental equity research support for Portfolio Manager
scope.business_model: B2B and B2C asset management (institutional and individual investors)
primary_metrics_inferred: Quality and accuracy of financial models and research
Promotion potential to Investment Analyst within 3-5 years
core_responsibilities: Provide fundamental research support to the Large-Cap Growth Strategy Portfolio Manager
Collaboration with investment analysts and portfolio managers
Extensive financial analysis and company/industry related research projects
Incorporating macroeconomic and market analysis into bottom-up company research to support investment decisions
Interact with public company C-suite executives
Provide investment insight and impact the investment process beyond just number crunching

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($110K-$160K base) and consistent across all geographies
Structured 3-5 year development program with mentoring and training
Clear promotion path to Investment Analyst for exceptional performers
Access to senior investment professionals and C-suite executives
Discretionary bonus eligibility on top of base salary
Generous benefits package including retirement plan and hybrid work schedule
red_flags: Hybrid policy is very limited — only up to one day per week from home, essentially near-full on-site commitment in Baltimore, MD
Work authorization requirement explicitly excludes H1-B, F-1 (OPT), TN, and other non-immigrant visa holders, with no sponsorship
Role is investment research, not product/program management — archetype 'Other' applied; may not match PM-focused applicants
No maximum years of experience stated — seniority expectations for applicants remain somewhat ambiguous (likely new grad / early career given program structure)

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 'three-to-five-year structured program', 'designed to develop future fundamental investment analysts', and Bachelor's degree requirement)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities and program structure)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description as asset manager serving individuals and institutions)
location.cities_listed (inferred from URL showing Baltimore-MD; body says 'Please review the job posting for the location')
compensation.location_pay_differential (ranges are identical across all listed geographies, so differential is false)
ambiguities: The JD lists identical salary ranges ($110K-$160K) for Maryland/Colorado/Washington/remote, Washington D.C., and New York/California — unusual given cost-of-living differences; unclear if this is intentional or a template artifact.

The JD mentions 'remote workers' in the salary range section but the work flexibility section says hybrid with 'up to one day per week from home' — treated as hybrid/onsite here; 'remote workers' reference may be a templated pay disclosure.

Most 'Preferred' qualifications read more like cultural/behavioral expectations that many would consider near-requirements (e.g., intellectual curiosity, passion for equity investing) — flagged but kept as soft per JD language.
